stories.title: Jewish man stabbed in Manhattan by assailant allegedly yelling ‘Allahu Akbar’
stories.summary: A 51-year-old man, Raul Morales, has been arrested in connection with the stabbing of two men on Manhattan's Upper West Side, with one victim being Jewish and the other Asian. The stabbings occurred near Central Park West at around 1:31 p.m. on Thursday, with both victims being transported to a nearby hospital in stable condition. Police are investigating the incident as a potential hate crime, with Morales allegedly yelling "Allahu Akbar" during the attacks.

stories.title: Alex Bowman announces retirement following 2027 racing season
stories.summary: NASCAR driver Alex Bowman has announced his retirement from full-time racing following the 2027 season, at the age of 33, after signing a one-year extension with Hendrick Motorsports to stay with the team in 2027. Bowman, who has eight Cup Series wins and seven playoff appearances, cited the importance of listening to his body and making the right choices for himself, particularly after battling vertigo earlier this season. He aims to close his career on a high note with the team and their fans.

stories.title: Heisman Trophy runner-up Diego Pavia cut by Ravens - Chicago Sun-Times
stories.summary: The Baltimore Ravens have waived quarterback Diego Pavia, the Heisman Trophy runner-up from last season at Vanderbilt, just a day before rookies were to report to training camp. Pavia, who signed as an undrafted free agent in April, had hoped to compete for the third quarterback spot behind Lamar Jackson and Tyler Huntley. He will now become a free agent if he goes unclaimed on waivers, open to sign with any team.

stories.title: Concacaf Confirms Pots and Procedures for 2026/27 Concacaf Nations League Draw
stories.summary: Concacaf has confirmed the draw pots and procedures for the 2026/27 Concacaf Nations League, with the live draw taking place on Thursday, July 23, at 7 p.m. ET in Miami, Florida, to determine the groups for Leagues A, B, and C. The draw will be conducted using the Concacaf Men's National Team Ranking as of July 20, 2026, with the Confederation's 41 participating Member Associations allocated into the League A, B, and C draw pots based on their ranking. The four highest-ranked teams in League A, Mexico, the United States, Canada, and Panama, will automatically advance to the quarterfinals.

stories.title: Dozens of Howard University students unenrolled weeks before fall semester
stories.summary: Dozens of Howard University students have been unenrolled from the school just weeks before the fall semester, with over 500 students receiving emails stating they missed a July 10 payment deadline. However, many students claim they are still waiting on outside scholarships and financial aid that won't be posted until August 1, and are asking the university to reconsider their decision. The students are now facing uncertainty about their academic plans, with some having already packed their belongings to move into campus housing.

stories.title: Cynthia Cooper and Teresa Weatherspoon Select Rosters for 2026 AT&T WNBA All-Star Game
stories.summary: Cynthia Cooper and Teresa Weatherspoon have selected the rosters for the 2026 AT&T WNBA All-Star Game, with the game to be played on Saturday, July 25 at the United Center in Chicago, broadcast on ABC at 8:30 p.m. ET. The teams will compete for a $100,000 prize pool benefiting two Chicago-based organizations dedicated to keeping girls involved in sports. The game marks the culmination of a multi-day, multiplatform coverage of AT&T WNBA All-Star 2026 in Chicago.

stories.title: Bandai Namco Announces ‘Mobile Suit Gundam RG XARX-ZERO’ and New "RG Project" Vision
stories.summary: Bandai Namco has announced a new multimedia universe, 'Mobile Suit Gundam RG XARX-ZERO,' set to launch in 2027, with a shared universe that directly links to the upcoming action video game 'Gundam: Rogue Orbit.' The anime series, produced by SUNRISE and animated by SOLA ANIMATION, is helmed by Kenji Kamiyama and serves as the foundational narrative pillar of the 'RG Project.' This new era for the legendary mecha franchise is expected to create a cohesive media-mix experience.

stories.title: Marvel Tokon's First DLC Character Will Be Phoenix Cyclops
stories.summary: Marvel's Wolverine game will feature Phoenix Cyclops as its first DLC character, a villainous version of Cyclops taken over by the Phoenix Force, as seen in the Avengers vs X-Men storyline. This character is set to be part of the game's story, with a new trailer introducing Jean Grey as a love interest for Wolverine. The trailer does not provide gameplay details or a release date for Phoenix Cyclops.

stories.title: UK complacent about war threat, warns defence boss
stories.summary: The UK's defence boss has warned of a heightened threat of foreign attack, with the level of risk being the highest in his lifetime. According to defence contractor BAE Systems, the UK needs to significantly increase its defence spending to meet the government's 3.5% of GDP commitment by 2035. This comes as Russia and Ukraine have become increasingly adept at using autonomous drones, posing a significant challenge to the UK's defence capabilities.

stories.title: China's Moonshot AI stole from Anthropic, Trump tech adviser says
stories.summary: China's Moonshot AI has been accused of stealing capabilities from top US artificial intelligence model Fable AI, developed by Anthropic, through a process called distillation, according to US President Donald Trump's Science and Technology adviser Michael Kratsios. The alleged theft allowed Moonshot to develop its K3 model, which was unveiled last week and is said to rival top US technology. The US government claims to have information about Moonshot's actions, which could have significant implications for the global AI landscape.

stories.title: ‘Batman: Caped Crusader’ Season 2 Reveals Joker Voice Actor: ‘House of the Dragon’ Star Matthew Needham
stories.summary: Here's a brief news report based on the given information: Batman: Caped Crusader's Season 2 has revealed its Joker voice actor as Matthew Needham, a star from the hit series House of the Dragon. Needham will take over the role from John DiMaggio, who voiced the Joker in the final episode of Season 1. The 10-episode second season of the animated series will premiere on Amazon Prime Video on July 31.

stories.title: Google fined €890m by EU for favouring its own apps over rivals
stories.summary: Google has been fined €890m by the EU for allegedly abusing its power by favouring its own apps and services over those from rivals, in the first major action under the EU's Digital Markets Act. The Commission argued this limited consumer choice and gave Google's services an unfair advantage, with a €460m penalty for one breach and €430m for another. Google will now have to strip away features from its services used by millions of European customers to comply with the EU's requirements.

stories.title: Traverse City 'Big Brother' houseguest faces elimination on Thursday
stories.summary: Rome Seymour, a 28-year-old houseguest from Traverse City, is at risk of being evicted from the CBS reality series "Big Brother 28" on Thursday's live episode, alongside Melody Morris and Jason De Puy. Seymour was nominated for eviction after being targeted by Head of Household Rick Devens, who replaced Lyric Medeiros with him in the nomination process. The remaining two houseguests will have their fate decided by a vote of their fellow houseguests on Thursday's episode.
stories.trend: what time is big brother on tonight

stories.title: US imposes tariffs on dozens of trade partners over 'forced labour' imports
stories.summary: The US has imposed tariffs ranging from 10% to 12.5% on around 60 trading partners, accounting for the majority of its imports, over claims they failed to stop forced labour. The targeted countries include key economic partners such as the UK, China, EU, Canada, Japan, and India, with the duties taking effect on Friday. This move marks the latest escalation in the global trade war, as the US seeks to address human rights abuses and distortive trade practices.

stories.title: Charli xcx Cranks Up the Guitars and Launches Her Post-‘Brat’ Era With the Razor-Sharp ‘Music, Fashion, Film’: Album Review
stories.summary: Charli xcx has launched her post-'Brat' era with the album 'Music, Fashion, Film', marking her seventh studio release. The album follows her self-aware mockumentary 'The Moment', which explored the pressures of stardom in the 21st century. With this new project, Charli xcx continues to push boundaries in the entertainment industry, blending music, fashion, and film in a bold and innovative way.

stories.title: Banana Ball World Tour comes to Hartford starting tonight
stories.summary: Tonight, the Banana Ball World Tour kicks off in Hartford, Connecticut, with sold-out games scheduled for July 23-25 at Dunkin' Park, featuring the Party Animals and the Indianapolis Clowns, two teams that play the unique style of baseball made famous by the Savannah Bananas. The tour brings this entertaining format to the city, offering a fun twist on traditional baseball. Fans who missed out on tickets will have to wait for future tour stops.

stories.title: US health panel recommends easing restrictions on controversial peptides
stories.summary: A US health panel has narrowly recommended easing restrictions on several peptides, including BPC-157, TB-500 and KPV, despite warnings from government scientists that these chemicals have not been proven safe or effective. The panel voted 8-6, with one abstention, to put the peptides on the FDA's list of substances considered safe for pharmacy compounding. If the FDA follows this advice, telehealth companies are expected to increase marketing of the peptides for wellness and cosmetic uses.

stories.title: Shaking Up the Emmys By Moving Five Categories Out of the Telecast Might Not Work, But at Least the TV Academy Is Trying Something
stories.summary: The TV Academy has voted to move five Emmy categories out of the main telecast, affecting nominees such as Jason Bateman, Dakota Fanning, and David Harbour, who will now attend multiple ceremonies on September 5 and 6, in addition to the main event on September 14. This change affects categories including writing for a variety series, supporting actor and actress in a limited series or movie, and directing for a limited series or movie. The shift aims to streamline the Emmy ceremonies, but may not necessarily address concerns about the show's format.

stories.title: Judge Extends Pause for Paramount-Warner Bros. Deal
stories.summary: The court blocked the companies from closing the acquisition through mid-August, as a judge considers two lawsuits challenging it.

stories.title: Hull KR inflict more derby misery on Hull FC
stories.summary: Hull KR inflicted more derby misery on Hull FC, winning 28-20 in a Super League match that saw the Robins extend their winning run to nine against their city rivals. The away side came from behind to take the lead, with tries from Tom Amone, Arthur Mourgue, Jack Broadbent, Joe Burgess, and Jordan Dezaria securing the victory. Hull FC's losing streak now stands at six straight losses, with the struggling side currently second-bottom in the Super League table.

stories.title: King's Tardis entrance as Commonwealth Games begin
stories.summary: The Commonwealth Games have officially begun in Glasgow, Scotland, with a grand opening ceremony featuring a rousing rendition of Scotland's unofficial anthem, "Yes Sir, I Can Boogie," performed by singer Callum Beattie, and a lively dance by the Scottish athletes. The ceremony also included a theatrical performance billed as a love letter to Glasgow and the Commonwealth, and a showstopping appearance by the Scotland team, who entered the arena in their white shirts and kilts to a thunderous applause. The event is expected to draw large crowds over the next 11 days.

stories.title: Iran Rejects U.S. Cease-Fire Deal Presented by Iraqi Leader
stories.summary: The details of the proposal were not immediately clear, but Iranian officials said that it was the only offer on the table.

stories.title: India activist ends hunger strike after 26 days
stories.summary: India activist Sonam Wangchuk has ended his 26-day hunger strike after the government assured him no legal action would be taken against protesters rallying at Janta Mantar site. This decision comes after state and health ministers visited Wangchuk in hospital. The protests, led by the Cockroach Janta Party, will continue until Education Minister Dharmendra Pradhan resigns, with mass demonstrations still ongoing in the Indian capital.

stories.title: Trump’s New Demand for Nuclear Deal Puts Saudis in a Bind
stories.summary: Saudi Arabia has long said that a condition of normalizing relations with Israel would be a pathway toward Palestinian statehood, a prospect that Israel’s current government has rejected.

stories.title: U.S. Withdraws Subpoenas Issued to New York Times Journalists
stories.summary: A federal judge raised questions about the government’s handling of the subpoenas, which The Times called an attempt to intimidate the free press.

stories.title: Health Care Agency Discontinues Dozens of Active Research Awards
stories.summary: The Agency for Healthcare Research and Quality slowed the awarding of new grants last year. Now it is stopping existing ones.

stories.title: Google and Tesla shares plunge as AI spending rattles markets
stories.summary: Shares of Google and Tesla plummeted on Thursday, with Alphabet's parent company experiencing a nearly 7% drop and Tesla's stock falling 14.5% as investors express concern over massive AI spending. The tech giants reported negative free cash flow in financial results, with Alphabet now expecting to spend up to $205 billion this year on AI projects and infrastructure. This surge in spending has left investors wondering when financial benefits will materialize, sparking a wave of skepticism about the return on investment.

stories.title: Should Travelers Give Up on Summer in Western Europe Because of the Heat?
stories.summary: A favorite vacation option may be at risk because of rising temperatures. Even those sticking with their summer plans agree: More air conditioning would help.

stories.title: Microsoft responds to LG monitors installing McAfee ads on Windows
stories.summary: Microsoft has responded to concerns over LG monitors installing McAfee ads on Windows without user permission, with the company stating that it will investigate the matter and review its policies on peripheral apps. The issue affects users who have purchased LG monitors, some of which cost over $1,000, and have been installing the LG Monitor App Installer via Windows Update. The app has been collecting user data, including geolocation and online activity, without explicit consent.

stories.title: A Peptide Showdown: F.D.A. Scientists May Clash With R.F.K.’s Agenda
stories.summary: In an all-day hearing, the agency’s scientists argued against allowing broad access to unproven drugs that the agency had banned three years ago.

stories.title: Intel Benefits From a New Shift in A.I. Spending
stories.summary: The Silicon Valley chipmaker’s revenue rose 25 percent in the latest quarter, its fastest growth in 15 years, as A.I. firms increasingly bought chips known as central processing units.

stories.title: Number of U.S. Troop Deaths in Iran War Appears Lower on Pentagon Website
stories.summary: Three military officials and a Pentagon spokesman gave conflicting explanations for why the number of Americans killed in the Iran war had changed.

stories.title: BBC Inside Science
stories.summary: The US military will begin screening military personnel aged 30 and older for testosterone deficiency, with those found to have low levels offered voluntary hormone replacement therapy. The move aims to maintain a "healthy, capable, and decisively dominant fighting force", according to Secretary of Defence Pete Hegseth. This development is set to be closely monitored by medical experts, who will assess its effectiveness and potential consequences.

stories.title: Key Republican Senator Says He Will Support Erica Schwartz for C.D.C. Director
stories.summary: Senator Bill Cassidy, who is believed to hold the deciding vote in the Senate health committee for Dr. Erica Schwartz’s confirmation, had declined for days to say whether he would back her.

stories.title: The only way to stop petrol pump thieves | Brief letters
stories.summary: Petrol pump thieves are a growing concern in the UK, with some stations now requiring customers to insert their payment card or make a cash deposit before releasing the pump handle. This measure aims to prevent individuals from filling up and then fleeing without paying. As a result, some garage owners are adopting this practice to minimize losses and reduce the risk of theft.
